faq.fullPageSubtitle: Everything you need to know about deploying, securing, and scaling AI agent fleets with OpenLegion.
faq.heading: Frequently asked

0.answer: No. The managed hosting at app.openlegion.ai requires no coding — sign up, pick a template, add your LLM API key, and your agents are live in minutes. The self-hosted version requires Python 3.10+ and Docker. Either way, the built-in team templates (Dev Team, Sales Pipeline, Content Studio) work out of the box with no configuration needed.
0.question: Do I need to be a developer to use OpenLegion?

1.answer: Every paid subscription includes a welcome bundle of LLM credits that never expire — use them from day one with no API keys needed. You can also bring your own API keys from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or any of 100+ supported providers and pay those providers directly with zero markup on model usage.
1.question: Do I pay for LLM usage on top of the subscription?

10.answer: An AI agent framework is the runtime and library layer for deploying, coordinating, and governing autonomous AI agents in production. Unlike raw SDK libraries that only provide agent logic primitives, a framework like OpenLegion handles container isolation, credential vaulting, per-agent cost controls, observability, and deployment — so teams can ship agents without building DevOps from scratch.
10.question: What is an AI agent framework?

11.answer: A framework is a code library and runtime for building and running agent logic — tools, prompts, memory, coordination. Managed hosting adds an operational layer on top: dedicated VPS provisioning, dashboard, billing, and credit proxy. OpenLegion is both: a Python framework (container-isolated multi-agent runtime) for authoring and running agents, and an optional managed hosting service for teams that don't want to manage infrastructure.
11.question: What is an AI agent framework vs managed hosting?

12.answer: OpenLegion uses a fleet model — blackboard + pub/sub + handoff (no CEO agent). Agents coordinate through a SQLite-backed blackboard with atomic compare-and-set, a pub/sub event bus, and a structured handoff protocol. Each agent has its own permissions, budget, and tool set. Users talk to agents directly; there is no LLM in the control plane deciding task routing.
12.question: How does agent coordination work in OpenLegion?

13.answer: AI agent security addresses the unique threats autonomous agents introduce: credential leakage, prompt injection, resource abuse, and data exfiltration. OpenLegion's defense-in-depth — container isolation, credential vault proxy, per-agent permission matrix, input sanitization, SSRF protection, and Unicode/path-traversal hardening — mitigates each threat independently, so a breach in one layer does not compromise the others.
13.question: What does AI agent security mean for autonomous agents?

2.answer: Any task a human performs on a computer with a browser or terminal. Agents can browse and interact with any website, log into web applications, fill out forms, extract data from any page, send emails and messages, manage files and folders, write and execute code, process documents, post to social platforms, monitor pages for changes, and run custom automations — all 24/7 without supervision. Common deployments include sales outreach pipelines, competitive research, lead qualification, developer workflows, invoice processing, content production, and internal task automation.
2.question: What kinds of tasks can OpenLegion agents actually automate?

3.answer: OpenLegion is a production-grade, container-isolated multi-agent runtime that deploys autonomous agent fleets in isolated Docker containers. Each agent gets its own budget, permissions, and credential vault — with defense-in-depth via container isolation, vault proxy, per-agent ACLs, and bounded execution. It requires only Python, Docker, and an API key. No Redis, no Kubernetes, no LangChain. Licensed under PolyForm Perimeter License 1.0.1 (source-available).
3.question: What is OpenLegion?

4.answer: OpenLegion container-isolates every agent, proxies all credentials through a vault, and enforces per-agent budgets — most frameworks don't. CrewAI and similar frameworks run agents in shared processes with no isolation, no cost controls, and API keys stored in config files. OpenLegion uses a fleet model (no CEO agent) with blackboard coordination and structured handoff — no LLM in the control plane deciding task routing.
4.question: How is OpenLegion different from CrewAI or other agent frameworks?

5.answer: 100+ LLM providers through LiteLLM. This includes Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI (GPT), Google (Gemini), Mistral, Moonshot, and any OpenAI-compatible API. You can assign different models to different agents in the same fleet — no vendor lock-in.
5.question: What LLM providers does OpenLegion support?

6.answer: Through the vault proxy — agents never see API keys. Keys are held in the mesh process on the mesh host. When an agent calls an LLM, the request goes through a vault proxy that injects the credential at the network layer, tracks token usage, and enforces budget limits. Even a fully compromised agent cannot access your API keys.
6.question: How does OpenLegion handle API key security?

7.answer: No. OpenLegion runs on a single machine with no external services. You need only Python 3.10+, Docker, and an LLM API key — no Redis, no Kubernetes, no LangChain, no external databases.
7.question: Do I need Kubernetes or cloud infrastructure to run OpenLegion?

8.answer: Yes — OpenLegion is designed for production deployment. It includes on-premises support, a fleet model with blackboard + pub/sub + handoff coordination, per-agent cost governance, a per-agent permission matrix, credential isolation via vault proxy, and an auditable codebase of ~77,000 lines with 5,800+ tests. Defense-in-depth controls are enabled by default.
8.question: Can I run OpenLegion in production?

9.answer: Yes. The engine runs on a single machine with Python + Docker, no external services required. For fully local inference, point the LLM provider at Ollama; for hosted models (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) the mesh proxy reaches out to whichever provider you configure. Mesh host, agents, vault, and dashboard all run on the same machine.
9.question: Can OpenLegion run on-premises?

afterRefund.heading: What happens to your account after a refund

items.0: Your subscription is cancelled and your hosted instance is deprovisioned shortly after the refund is processed.
items.1: Welcome credits granted with the subscription are reclaimed and your credit balance returns to zero (consistent with the refund).
items.2: You are not eligible to claim a second money-back refund or a second welcome-credits grant on a future subscription. Future subscriptions are billed normally.

items.0: Your first subscription payment (the actual amount charged — e.g. $19 for Basic monthly).
items.1: Full refund of that amount — no questions asked, no partial pro-ration.

howTo.body: Email {email} with your account email and subscription ID. Refunds are processed within 5 business days via Polar (funds return to your original payment method).
howTo.heading: How to request a refund

moneyBackPage.intro: We stand behind OpenLegion. If your first month isn't a fit for any reason, email {email} within 7 days of your first subscription payment and we'll refund the full amount you actually paid (the launch-pricing amount billed by Polar — not the higher anchor displayed alongside).

items.0: Credit top-ups (already consumed credits are non-refundable — unused credits remain on your account until the refund is processed).
items.1: Renewal billing after the first 7 days.
items.2: Subscriptions used abusively (violating the acceptable use policy).

moneyBackPage.tagline: Full refund on your first month, no questions asked.

0.answer: Every paid plan includes a welcome credits bundle and full access on day one — VPS, system proxy, and the operator dashboard. Not a fit? Cancel within 7 days and we refund your first month, no questions asked. Prefer to bring your own API keys? You can, and the welcome credits are yours to keep.
0.question: What does the plan include on day one?

1.answer: Every paid subscription includes a welcome bonus of LLM credits that never expire. Once your first payment processes, use credits to power your agents — no API keys needed. Prefer to use your own API keys from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or 100+ other providers? You can do that from day one and skip credits entirely. Need more credits later? Top up anytime from $5.
1.question: Do I need to pay for LLM usage on top of the plan price?

2.answer: Yes — upgrade any time and your new limits apply immediately. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing period.
2.question: Can I switch plans?

3.answer: After cancellation you retain access until your current billing period ends. After that, your VPS is deprovisioned and instance data is removed.
3.question: What happens to my agents if I cancel?

4.answer: Yes. Clone the repo, run three commands, and you have a full agent fleet on your own infrastructure. The engine is source-available under PolyForm Perimeter License 1.0.1. Managed hosting starts at $19/month for teams that don't want to manage infrastructure.
4.question: Can I self-host OpenLegion?

5.answer: Each deployed agent container counts as one agent toward your plan limit. Templates like Dev Team (PM + Engineer + Reviewer) count as 3 agents.
5.question: What counts as an "agent"?

6.answer: Credits are used to pay for LLM calls (Claude, GPT, Gemini, etc.) through our platform. You get a welcome bonus when your first payment processes — Basic gets $15, Growth gets $50, Pro gets $100, Pro Max gets $200. Credits never expire. When they run out, top up any amount from $5 to $500, or add your own API keys to use providers directly.
6.question: What are LLM credits and do they expire?

privacy.effectiveDate: Effective date: March 7, 2026 · Last updated: March 7, 2026

section1.p1: This Privacy Policy describes how OpenLegion ("we," "us," "our") collects, uses, and discloses information when you visit openlegion.ai (the "Website") or use our managed hosting service, APIs, and related services (collectively, the "Service").
section1.p2: By using the Service, you consent to the practices described in this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.
section1.title: 1. Introduction

section10.text: If you are a California resident, you have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), including the right to know what personal information we collect, request deletion, and opt out of the sale of personal information. We do not sell personal information. To exercise your CCPA rights, contact support@openlegion.ai.
section10.title: 10. California Residents (CCPA)

section11.p1: If you are in the EEA, our legal basis for processing your personal data includes: (a) your consent; (b) performance of a contract; (c) compliance with a legal obligation; and (d) our legitimate interests (improving and securing the Service) where those interests are not overridden by your data protection rights.
section11.p2: You may lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority if you believe we have violated your rights under the GDPR.
section11.title: 11. European Economic Area (GDPR)

section12.text: Your information may be transferred to and processed in the United States or other countries where our service providers operate. These countries may have different data protection laws than your jurisdiction. By using the Service, you consent to such transfers.
section12.title: 12. International Data Transfers

section13.text: The Service is not directed to individuals under 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that we have collected data from a child, we will delete it promptly.
section13.title: 13. Children's Privacy

section14.text: We may update this Privacy Policy at any time. Changes will be reflected by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Your continued use of the Service after any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated Privacy Policy.
section14.title: 14. Changes to This Policy

section15.text: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your rights, contact us at:
section15.title: 15. Contact Us

items.0: Account information: Email address, name, and password when you create an account.
items.1: Payment information: Billing details processed by our third-party payment processor (Polar). We do not store full credit card numbers on our servers.
items.2: Communications: Information you provide when contacting us via email, live chat (Tawk.to), or Discord.
items.3: Customer Data: Agent configurations, workflow definitions, and other data you submit to the Service.

items.0: Usage data: Pages visited, features used, timestamps, referral URLs, and general interaction patterns.
items.1: Device information: Browser type, operating system, screen resolution, and device identifiers.
items.2: IP address: Collected for security, analytics, and approximate geolocation purposes.
items.3: Cookies and similar technologies: See Section 6 below.

section3.intro: We use the information we collect to:

items.0: Provide, operate, and maintain the Service;
items.1: Process payments and send billing-related communications;
items.2: Respond to your inquiries and provide customer support;
items.3: Analyze usage patterns to improve the Service (via Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity);
items.4: Detect, prevent, and address technical issues, fraud, or abuse;
items.5: Send transactional emails (account confirmation, password reset, billing receipts);

section3.noSell: We do not sell your personal information. We do not use Customer Data to train AI models.
section3.title: 3. How We Use Your Information

section4.intro: We use the following third-party services that may collect information as described in their respective privacy policies:

items.0: Google Analytics — website analytics and traffic measurement.
items.1: Microsoft Clarity — session recording and heatmaps for UX analysis.
items.2: Tawk.to — live chat widget for customer support.
items.4: LLM API providers (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral) — AI model inference. Prompts and agent data sent to these providers are governed by their respective privacy policies and data processing terms. OpenLegion is not responsible for how third-party LLM providers handle data.

section4.review: We encourage you to review the privacy policies of these third-party services.
section4.title: 4. Third-Party Services

section5.intro: We may share your information only in the following circumstances:

items.0: Service providers: Third-party vendors who assist in operating the Service (hosting, payment processing, analytics) under contractual obligations to protect your data.
items.1: Legal requirements: When required by law, subpoena, court order, or governmental request.
items.2: Protection of rights: To enforce our Terms of Service, protect our rights or safety, or investigate fraud.
items.3: Business transfers: In connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, your information may be transferred as part of that transaction.

section5.title: 5. Data Sharing & Disclosure

section6.control: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Disabling cookies may affect the functionality of the Service.
section6.intro: We use cookies and similar technologies (pixels, local storage) for analytics, functionality, and security purposes. The Website uses:

items.0: Essential cookies: Required for the Service to function (session management, authentication).
items.1: Analytics cookies: Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity cookies to understand how visitors interact with the Website.
items.2: Third-party cookies: Set by Tawk.to for live chat functionality.

section6.title: 6. Cookies & Tracking Technologies

section7.text: We retain your information for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide the Service. We may also retain information as necessary to comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements. When data is no longer needed, we will delete or anonymize it within a reasonable timeframe.
section7.title: 7. Data Retention

section8.text: We implement commercially reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your information. However, no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security and are not liable for any unauthorized access, data breach, or loss of data.
section8.title: 8. Data Security

section9.contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at support@openlegion.ai. We will respond within 30 days (or as required by applicable law).
section9.intro: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal information:

items.0: Access: Request a copy of the personal information we hold about you.
items.1: Correction: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
items.2: Deletion: Request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal retention requirements.
items.3: Portability: Request your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
items.4: Objection: Object to processing of your personal information for certain purposes.
items.5: Opt-out of sale: We do not sell personal information. If this changes, we will provide a clear opt-out mechanism.

section9.title: 9. Your Rights

security.allLayersLink: All six layers explained in detail →
security.heading: AI agent security: built assuming agents will

0.description: Docker containers or Docker Sandbox microVMs per agent — no shared process space.
0.title: Each agent runs in its own container

1.description: Non-root user (UID 1000), no-new-privileges flag, memory and CPU resource limits enforced.
1.title: Agents can't escalate privileges or consume your resources

2.description: Vault proxy holds API keys and wallet private keys — agents call through the proxy, never see secrets. Transactions are signed server-side.
2.title: API keys and wallet keys live in a vault the agent never touches

3.description: Per-agent ACL matrix controls which tools, files, and mesh operations are allowed.
3.title: Each agent only accesses what you explicitly allow

4.description: Path traversal prevention, SSRF protection, safe condition evaluation, token budget enforcement.
4.title: Malicious inputs blocked before they reach the LLM

5.description: Invisible character stripping at 56 choke points — bidi overrides, tag chars, zero-width chars blocked before reaching LLM context.
5.title: Hidden characters stripped before prompt injection hits

security.subtitle: Defense-in-depth enabled by default. Six layers shown below.

architecture.title: Defense-in-depth — trust zones at every layer
architecture.summary: Your multi-agent fleet runs across four trust tiers — untrusted external input, sandboxed agents, the trusted mesh, and a loopback-only internal tier — plus an operator-or-internal permission tier. Agents only message each other through the mesh, gated by per-agent ACLs; the mesh holds the keys.
architecture.learnMoreLink: Learn more about AI agent orchestration →

technicalDefinition.label: Technical definition
technicalDefinition.text: OpenLegion is a container-isolated multi-agent runtime with credential vault proxy, per-agent budgets, and a fleet coordination model (blackboard + pub/sub + handoff, no CEO agent) — designed for teams that need secure, cost-controlled agent fleets in production.

section12.title: 12. Indemnification

section13.text: We do not guarantee any specific uptime or availability. The Service may be modified, suspended, or discontinued at any time, with or without notice. We are not liable to you or any third party for any modification, suspension, or discontinuation of the Service.
section13.title: 13. Service Availability & Modifications

section14.p1: We may terminate or suspend your access to the Service immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason, including breach of these Terms. Upon termination, your right to use the Service ceases immediately. Sections that by their nature should survive termination (including, without limitation, Sections 10, 11, 12, 16, and 17) shall survive.
section14.p2: You may terminate your account at any time by discontinuing use of the Service. We are under no obligation to retain your data after termination.
section14.title: 14. Termination

section15.text: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Your continued use of the Service after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. It is your responsibility to review these Terms periodically.
section15.title: 15. Modifications to Terms

section16.p1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States, without regard to its conflict-of-law provisions. Any dispute arising under or in connection with these Terms shall be resolved exclusively through binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) under its Commercial Arbitration Rules. The arbitration shall take place remotely (via telephone, video conference, or written submissions) unless both parties agree otherwise. The arbitrator's award shall be final and binding and may be entered as a judgment in any court of competent jurisdiction.
section16.p2: YOU AGREE THAT ANY DISPUTE RESOLUTION PROCEEDINGS WILL BE CONDUCTED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS AND NOT IN A CLASS, CONSOLIDATED, OR REPRESENTATIVE ACTION. If this class-action waiver is found to be unenforceable, then the entirety of this arbitration provision shall be null and void.
section16.title: 16. Governing Law & Dispute Resolution

items.0: Entire Agreement. These Terms, together with our Privacy Policy, constitute the entire agreement between you and OpenLegion.
items.1: Severability. If any provision of these Terms is held invalid or unenforceable, that provision shall be enforced to the maximum extent permissible, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force.
items.2: Waiver. No waiver of any term shall be deemed a further or continuing waiver of such term or any other term.

items.4: Force Majeure. OpenLegion shall not be liable for any failure or delay caused by events beyond our reasonable control, including natural disasters, war, terrorism, pandemics, labor disputes, government actions, power failures, internet or telecommunications failures, or third-party service outages.

section2.text: OpenLegion provides an AI agent framework and optional managed hosting platform for deploying, orchestrating, and monitoring autonomous AI agents. The Service may include web dashboards, APIs, container orchestration, third-party LLM provider integrations, and related tooling.
section2.title: 2. Description of Service

section3.text: You must be at least 18 years old and legally capable of entering into a binding agreement. The Service is not intended for use by anyone under the age of 18.
section3.title: 3. Eligibility

section4.text: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activity under your account. You agree to notify us immediately of any unauthorized access. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts at our sole discretion, with or without notice, for any reason, including violation of these Terms.
section4.title: 4. Accounts & Security

section5.enforcement: We may investigate violations and, at our sole discretion, immediately terminate or suspend your access without notice or refund.
section5.intro: You agree not to use the Service to:

items.0: Violate any applicable law, regulation, or third-party right;
items.1: Generate, distribute, or store content that is unlawful, harmful, threatening, abusive, defamatory, or otherwise objectionable;
items.2: Transmit malware, viruses, or any code designed to disrupt, damage, or limit the functionality of any software or hardware;
items.3: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Service, other accounts, or systems connected to the Service;
items.4: Use the Service for cryptocurrency mining, denial-of-service attacks, spam, or any activity that degrades performance for other users;
items.5: Reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble any proprietary component of the Service beyond what is permitted by the applicable open-source license;
items.6: Resell, sublicense, or redistribute the managed hosting Service without prior written consent;
items.7: Use AI agents deployed on the Service to engage in fraud, impersonation, harassment, or any illegal activity.

section5.title: 5. Acceptable Use

section6.p1: Certain features of the Service may require payment. All fees are stated on our pricing page and are billed in advance on a recurring basis. Fees are non-refundable except where required by applicable law. We reserve the right to change pricing at any time with 30 days' notice. Continued use after a price change constitutes acceptance of the new pricing.
section6.p2: You are solely responsible for all third-party costs incurred through your use of the Service, including but not limited to LLM API provider charges, cloud infrastructure fees, and domain registration costs. OpenLegion is not liable for any third-party charges.

section8.text: The Service integrates with third-party LLM providers (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, and others). Your use of those providers is governed by their respective terms of service and privacy policies. OpenLegion makes no representations or warranties regarding any third-party service, and is not responsible for the output, accuracy, availability, or conduct of any third-party LLM or API.

section9.text: AI agents deployed through the Service operate autonomously based on your configuration. You are solely responsible for the actions, outputs, and consequences of any AI agents you deploy, configure, or operate using the Service. OpenLegion does not control, review, or endorse agent outputs and accepts no liability for damages arising from agent behavior, including but not limited to financial losses, data corruption, incorrect information, or unintended actions taken by agents.
section9.title: 9. AI Agent Output & Autonomous Actions

terms.title: Terms of Service

0.quote: We were spending $2,800/mo on a lead-research VA team. Replaced the whole thing with five OpenLegion agents in three days. They run 24/7, never call in sick, and frankly do better research than the humans did.

1.quote: I'm not technical at all. I wrote what I wanted in plain English — "follow up with leads who haven't replied in five days, sound friendly not pushy" — and the agent figured the rest out. Closed three deals this quarter from that one workflow.

2.quote: Two months in, the fleet runs our invoicing, content drafts, and customer research end-to-end. We saved enough on freelance hours to pay for Pro Max twice over. The hardest part was deciding what to automate first.

useCases.heading: Which AI agent team do you need —

0.description: OpenLegion's Dev Team template automates task planning, code generation, and PR review — the triage and boilerplate that eat hours of every engineer's week.
0.name: Your engineering team

1.description: OpenLegion's Sales Pipeline template runs lead research, scoring, and personalized outreach 24/7 — a full week of manual prospecting handled while you sleep.
1.name: Your sales team

2.description: OpenLegion's Content Studio template pairs a researcher and a writer with consistent brand voice — research-to-draft cycles that take days land in hours.
2.name: Your content team

3.description: Wallet-aware agents execute onchain — swaps, transfers, DeFi positions — while private keys stay locked in the vault and are signed server-side. Per-agent spend limits, daily caps, and EVM + Solana support.
3.name: Your treasury team

4.description: Don't see your use case? Define any combination of roles, tools, and budgets. Agents get their own containers, credentials, and spending limits — whatever the job requires.
4.name: Your custom team

useCases.subtitle: Pick a built-in multi-agent template or define your own autonomous agent fleet. Each agent gets its own container, budget, and tool permissions.
